evidence: Step 0: AI is Ready. Current models (Claude 3.5 Sonnet, GPT-4o, etc.) are already capable of writing, researching, and synthesizing better and faster than most people.
Step 1: Death of Junior Execution Roles. Repetitive, "text-in, text-out" roles with low autonomy will cease to exist.
Step 2: Rise of the Super IC. The new norm will be senior "Super Individual Contributors" who manage dozens of specialized AI agents. These generalists will span traditional boundaries.
Step 3: Small Teams > Big Teams. Lean teams of Super ICs will outperform large organizations because they bypass the "coordination tax".
Step 4: Elimination of Bureaucracy. Managers who exist solely to coordinate large numbers of people will become obsolete as the total headcount per project shrinks.
Step 5: The Future Company. High-agency, high-trust environments where small groups produce high-quality work with massive leverage.
Relay App operates with only 10 people but achieves the output of a 20+ person team. Jacob personally handles marketing, support, sales, HR, and finance by managing roughly 60 AI agents.

David shared personal stories about long-term planning through the lens of a 20-year streak of annual buddy trips, emphasizing the importance of a "forcing mechanism" and having a "Lewis" (an orchestrator/facilitator).
The "17-page manifesto" Vrijen sent Logan on the day ChatGPT was released, which kicked off their current venture 2.5 years ago.
Vrijen and Logan discussed the "dark joke" of being perpetually close to success but still "inching forward" in the startup grind.
